aliases: சோயா மில்க் ஃப்ளாட் வைட் (Soy Flat White)

tamil: சோயா மில்க் ஃப்ளாட் வைட் (Soy Flat White)

cuisine: Contemporary café — Antipodean (Australian/New Zealand) origin, Singapore adoption
region_of_origin: Australia/New Zealand (flat white origin); Singapore (soy milk variant mainstream)
food_subgroup: Coffee-based beverage — double espresso with steamed barista-grade soy milk, unsweetened

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for soy milk flat white. Closest parent is plant-based milk coffee beverage (FOODON:00002473 — coffee beverage). No exact URI assigned to avoid fabrication.
ontology.foodex2_description: Coffee-based beverages — closest FoodEx2 parent for espresso-based drinks with plant milk. Code AN000A (Coffee and coffee substitutes) used as closest defensible parent; no exact soy milk flat white code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for soy milk flat white. AN000A (coffee and coffee substitutes) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for soy milk flat white or barista soy milk coffee beverage.

main_ingredients: barista soy milk (Vitasoy Barista or Alpro Barista)

serving.description: 1 cup (270ml, approximately 280g) — double espresso 60ml + barista soy milk 200ml + minor foam volume
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Singapore café serving is approximately 270ml (280g), comprising a double espresso shot (60ml) topped with approximately 200ml of steamed and microfoamed barista-grade soy milk, unsweetened.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy — Soymilk (all flavors), unsweetened, with added calcium, vitamins A and D (FDC ID 1097542) + USDA espresso reference; composite weighted calculation for 200ml barista soy milk + 60ml espresso per 270ml serving
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service (ARS), FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Composite calculation: barista soy milk (200ml, ~74% of beverage by weight) weighted from USDA SR Legacy unsweetened fortified soymilk data, cross-referenced with Vitasoy Barista and Alpro Barista product label data. Double espresso (60ml, ~26% by weight) contributes negligible macronutrients but meaningful caffeine and chlorogenic acids. Per-100g values represent the blended beverage. Vitamin B12 and vitamin D values reflect fortification levels typical of barista soy milk products.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — soy milk (unsweetened) GI ~34; composite estimate for soy milk flat white ~40 accounting for espresso contribution
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: Unsweetened soy milk GI is reported at approximately 34 (low) in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 international GI tables. Barista soy milk contains a small amount of added rapeseed oil and slightly higher protein than standard soy milk, which further attenuates glycemic response. Composite GI for the flat white (~40) accounts for the minor dilution effect of espresso (negligible carbohydrate, GI not applicable). GL per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (40 × 15.4g) / 100 ≈ 6.2. Carbohydrate per 280g serving = 5.5 × 2.8 = 15.4g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (48mg/100g); moderate caffeine (~140mg per double shot); soy isoflavones with ACE-inhibitory properties
hypertension.guidance: Soy milk flat white is suitable for hypertensive patients — sodium is very low, soy isoflavones (genistein, daidzein) exert mild ACE-inhibitory and endothelial-protective effects, and habitual coffee drinkers develop tolerance to the transient caffeine-induced blood pressure rise (~5–10 mmHg for 2–4 hours); long-term regular coffee consumption is associated with reduced cardiovascular risk in epidemiological studies.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low GI (~40), low GL (~6.2 per serving), soy isoflavones improving insulin sensitivity, espresso chlorogenic acids attenuating postprandial glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Soy milk flat white is a significantly better coffee choice for T2DM patients than cow's milk flat white — GI ~40 (low), GL ~6.2 (low), soy milk provides only ~8g carbohydrate per 200ml versus ~12g for cow's milk, soy isoflavones improve insulin sensitivity, and espresso chlorogenic acids reduce postprandial glucose excursions; unsweetened preparation is essential to maintain this benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero cholesterol, very low saturated fat (0.2g/100g), soy isoflavones with LDL-lowering and endothelial-protective effects, antioxidant chlorogenic acids from espresso
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Soy milk flat white is cardioprotective relative to dairy-milk coffee — cholesterol is zero, saturated fat is negligible (0.2g/100g), soy protein and isoflavones are associated with modest LDL reduction (~3–5%) and improved endothelial function, and espresso polyphenols (chlorogenic acids) provide antioxidant benefit; habitual moderate coffee consumption (3–5 cups/day) is associated with reduced cardiovascular mortality in meta-analyses.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Soy isoflavones inhibiting NF-κB and TNF-alpha; vitamin B12 fortification relevant for methotrexate users
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Soy isoflavones (~30mg per 200ml barista soy milk) inhibit NF-κB and reduce TNF-alpha production, providing emerging anti-inflammatory benefit for RA; vitamin B12 fortification is clinically relevant for RA patients on methotrexate, which depletes B12 as a folate antagonist.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soy Milk Flat White (豆奶馥芮白, Dòunǎi Fùruìbái) is a popular dairy-free coffee beverage at Singapore's specialty cafés — a double espresso (60ml) topped with approximately 200ml of steamed and microfoamed barista-grade soy milk, served unsweetened in a 270ml cup. Barista soy milk formulations (Vitasoy Barista, Alpro Barista) are specifically engineered for coffee applications: they contain higher protein than standard soy milk and a small amount of rapeseed oil to prevent curdling when mixed with espresso's acidity and heat, and to produce stable, glossy microfoam suitable for latte art. The beverage has a smooth, creamy texture with a mild, slightly nutty soy flavour that complements the espresso's bitterness without the richness of cow's milk. Nutritionally, soy milk flat white is superior to cow's milk flat white for metabolic health: soy milk provides only ~8g carbohydrate per 200ml versus ~12g for cow's milk, GI is low (~40), and GL per serving is low (~6.2). Soy isoflavones (genistein, daidzein, ~30mg per 200ml) improve insulin sensitivity and exert mild ACE-inhibitory and anti-inflammatory effects. Espresso contributes chlorogenic acids that attenuate postprandial glucose excursions. The beverage is zero cholesterol, very low in saturated fat, and fortified with calcium, vitamin D, and vitamin B12. Key comparison: soy milk 4–5g carbohydrate/100ml versus oat milk 10–12g/100ml — soy milk is substantially better than oat milk for T2DM patients. Singapore's most common barista soy milks are Vitasoy Barista (widely available at NTUC FairPrice) and Alpro Barista (Cold Storage and specialty stores). The flat white format originated in Australia/New Zealand and became mainstream in Singapore's specialty coffee scene from approximately 2015.
common_user_queries: soy milk vs oat milk coffee
soy flat white GI glycemic index
best plant milk for diabetes coffee
soy flat white vs oat flat white

confidence_reason: Nutrient values derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy unsweetened fortified soymilk data, cross-referenced with Vitasoy Barista and Alpro Barista product label data. Composite weighted calculation for 200ml barista soy milk + 60ml espresso is well-supported. GI estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database soy milk reference (~34) with defensible upward adjustment to ~40 for the composite beverage. All values are internally consistent and plausible.
notes: Composite beverage: 200ml barista soy milk (~74% by weight) + 60ml double espresso (~26% by weight) = 270ml total, approximately 280g. Per-100g values represent the blended beverage. Cholesterol is confirmed zero — soy milk is a plant-based product containing no dietary cholesterol; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central soymilk entries. Vitamin C is confirmed zero for this preparation — neither soy milk nor espresso contributes meaningful vitamin C; confirmed per USDA FoodData Central. Trans fat is confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central soymilk data and Vitasoy Barista / Alpro Barista product labels. Monounsaturated fat (0.3g/100g) and polyunsaturated fat (0.8g/100g) estimated from USDA SR Legacy soymilk fatty acid profile; detailed fatty acid breakdown for barista-specific formulations not separately published. Water content (89.0g/100g) estimated from beverage composition (predominantly water-based). Vitamin B12 (0.45mcg/100g) and vitamin D (0.5mcg/100g) reflect fortification levels in barista soy milk products; values cross-referenced with Vitasoy Barista and Alpro Barista label data. GL per serving = (GI 40 × carbohydrate 15.4g per 280g serving) / 100 = 6.2. Caffeine content approximately 140mg per double espresso shot (not included in per_100g nutrient panel as caffeine is not a standard nutrient field). FoodEx2 code AN000A used as closest defensible parent (coffee and coffee substitutes); no exact code for soy milk flat white exists. FoodOn URI not assigned to avoid fabrication — closest parent is plant-based milk coffee beverage.
